title: Connect a SIP Provider
lede: Bring your own carrier to iMash. Connect any SIP trunk — Telnyx, Twilio, a wholesale carrier, or your on-prem PBX — and your AI agents can answer and place calls over the numbers you already own.

steps.title: Open the SIP Providers page
steps.content:
Most users never need this page — numbers you buy inside iMash come with calling built in. Connect a SIP provider only when you want to bring your own carrier and route calls through a trunk you already control.

  1. In the left sidebar, scroll to the Call Center section and click SIP Providers. (You can also get there from the top navigation: Contact Center → SIP Providers.)

The page shows every custom trunk you have connected, with these columns:

  • Label — the name you gave the trunk. Click it to open and edit the provider.
  • Title / Type — custom SIP providers show as Custom.
  • Status — Active means the trunk is live and usable.
  • Owner — which team member's workspace the trunk belongs to. Use the Filter by owner dropdown above the table to narrow the list.
  • Actions — the eye icon opens the provider, the trash icon deletes it.
💡 The API Info button in the top-right shows how to manage SIP providers programmatically — handy if you want to automate trunk setup later.

images.alt: SIP Providers list page with one connected trunk
images.caption: Call Center → SIP Providers lists every carrier trunk you have connected.

images.alt: SIP Providers list page with one connected trunk
images.caption: Call Center → SIP Providers lists every carrier trunk you have connected.

steps.title: Start a new provider
steps.content:
1. Click Add SIP Provider in the top-right of the page.

The Create SIP Provider panel slides out. Everything you need is on this one form:

  • Provider Name — a label so you can recognize the trunk later.
  • Have your carriers whitelist our SIP signaling IPs — the iMash address your carrier must allow.
  • Authentication — optional username/password, if your carrier requires it.
  • SIP gateways — where your carrier lives on the network (address, port, direction).

The next steps walk through each section.

images.caption: The Create SIP Provider panel — name, whitelist IP, authentication, and gateways in one form.
The Create SIP Provider panel — name, whitelist IP, authentication, and gateways in one form.

steps.title: Name the trunk and whitelist iMash with your carrier
steps.content:
1. Type a Provider Name — for example My Telnyx Trunk or Office PBX. This becomes the trunk's label everywhere in iMash.
  2. Open the panel labeled Have your carriers whitelist our SIP signaling IPs and copy the address shown: 34.150.248.33:5060.
  3. In your carrier's (or PBX's) admin portal, add that IP and port to the trunk's allowed / whitelisted IP list so it accepts SIP traffic from iMash and sends inbound calls to it.
⚠️ This whitelist step happens on your carrier's side, not in iMash. If it's skipped, calls will silently fail even though everything in iMash looks correct — it's the single most common reason a new trunk "doesn't work".

images.alt: iMash SIP signaling IP to whitelist
images.caption: Whitelist 34.150.248.33:5060 in your carrier's portal so it trusts iMash.

images.alt: iMash SIP signaling IP to whitelist
images.caption: Whitelist 34.150.248.33:5060 in your carrier's portal so it trusts iMash.

steps.title: Add authentication (only if your carrier requires it)
steps.content:
Many trunks authenticate purely by IP — if yours does, skip this step. If your carrier gave you SIP credentials:

  1. Tick the Authentication checkbox to expand the section.
  2. Enter the Auth username and Password exactly as your carrier provided them.
  3. Tick Require Auth for Incoming if the carrier expects credentials on calls coming into iMash.
  4. Tick Require Auth for Outgoing if it expects credentials on calls iMash sends out.
💡 Not sure which boxes to tick? Most credential-based trunks only need Require Auth for Outgoing — check your carrier's trunk settings page or ask their support.

images.alt: Authentication section with username, password and direction checkboxes
images.caption: Enable Authentication and choose which direction needs credentials.

images.alt: Authentication section with username, password and direction checkboxes
images.caption: Enable Authentication and choose which direction needs credentials.

steps.title: Configure your SIP gateways
steps.content:
A gateway tells iMash where your carrier lives on the network. You can add several Inbound gateways, but only one Outbound gateway.

  1. In the first gateway card, enter the Network address:
    • Inbound gateways need an IP address (for example 1.2.3.4) — this is the address your carrier sends calls from.
    • Outbound gateways accept an IP or a domain (for example sip.mycarrier.com) — this is where iMash sends your outgoing calls.
  2. Set the Port — 5060 is the SIP standard and almost always correct.
  3. Set the third field:
    • For Inbound gateways it's the Netmask — keep 32 for a single IP, or lower it to allow a whole range of carrier IPs.
    • For Outbound gateways it becomes the Protocol selector — AUTO, UDP, TCP, or TLS. Leave AUTO unless your carrier specifies one.
  4. Pick the direction with the Inbound / Outbound buttons on the card.
  5. Click Add New Gateway to add more (for example one inbound + one outbound), or Delete on a card to remove it.
💡 For two-way calling you typically need both: an inbound gateway with your carrier's signaling IP, and an outbound gateway pointing at your carrier's SIP domain.

images.alt: SIP gateway card with network address, port, netmask and direction buttons
images.caption: Each gateway card holds a network address, port, netmask/protocol, and a direction.

images.alt: SIP gateway card with network address, port, netmask and direction buttons
images.caption: Each gateway card holds a network address, port, netmask/protocol, and a direction.

images.alt: Inbound gateway card with netmask next to an outbound gateway card with the AUTO protocol selector
images.caption: An inbound + outbound pair: the outbound card swaps the netmask for a protocol selector (AUTO/UDP/TCP/TLS), and once one card is Outbound the others lock to Inbound.

images.alt: Inbound gateway card with netmask next to an outbound gateway card with the AUTO protocol selector
images.caption: An inbound + outbound pair: the outbound card swaps the netmask for a protocol selector (AUTO/UDP/TCP/TLS), and once one card is Outbound the others lock to Inbound.

steps.title: Create the provider and verify it
steps.content:
1. Review the form — the Create Provider button stays grayed out until the name is filled and every gateway has a valid address and port (and credentials are filled if Authentication is on).
  2. Click Create Provider.
  3. Back on the list, your trunk appears with a green Active status pill — that's your confirmation it was saved and is ready to carry numbers.

images.alt: SIP Providers list showing the new trunk with Active status
images.caption: Your new trunk appears in the list with an Active status.

images.alt: SIP Providers list showing the new trunk with Active status
images.caption: Your new trunk appears in the list with an Active status.

steps.title: Attach your phone numbers to the trunk
steps.content:
The trunk is connected — now give it numbers to carry. Calls only flow once at least one number is imported under this provider.

  1. In the left sidebar, click Phone numbers (under Call Center).
  2. Click Import Number.
  3. In the SIP Phone Provider dropdown, select the trunk you just created.
  4. Give the number a Label (for example Main line).
  5. Enter the Phone Number exactly as your SIP provider sends it — with or without the + prefix, e.g. +14155551234, 14155551234, or 14155551234#001. Untick Use country selector if you need to type a raw format.
  6. Click Import Phone Number. iMash provisions the call routing for the number automatically.
⚠️ Numbers on a custom SIP provider carry voice calls through your carrier. SMS on these numbers is managed by your external provider — iMash does not send or receive texts on them.

images.alt: Import Phone Number modal with SIP provider dropdown
images.caption: Import Number: pick your trunk, label the number, and type it exactly as your carrier sends it.

images.alt: Import Phone Number modal with SIP provider dropdown
images.caption: Import Number: pick your trunk, label the number, and type it exactly as your carrier sends it.

steps.title: Edit or remove a provider later
steps.content:
1. On the SIP Providers page, click the trunk's Label (or the eye icon under Actions).
  2. The SIP Provider Details panel opens with the same fields as creation — rename the trunk, toggle Authentication, or adjust the SIP gateways.
  3. Click Save to apply your changes, or Delete (confirm with Yes) to remove the trunk.
⚠️ Before deleting a trunk, move or remove any phone numbers imported under it — numbers without a provider cannot carry calls.

That's it — your own carrier is now wired into iMash, and every agent, campaign, and routing rule can use the numbers riding on it.

images.alt: SIP Provider Details edit panel with Save and Delete buttons
images.caption: Open a trunk anytime to adjust gateways or credentials — Save applies instantly.

images.alt: SIP Provider Details edit panel with Save and Delete buttons
images.caption: Open a trunk anytime to adjust gateways or credentials — Save applies instantly.

support.title: Need Help?
support.content:
If you run into any issues or have questions:

  • 📧 Email us at support@imash.io
  • 💬 Join our Discord community
  • 📚 Check out our API Reference
  • 🎥 Watch our video tutorials

metaTitle: Connect a SIP Provider - Bring Your Own Carrier | iMash Documentation
metaDescription: Connect your own SIP trunk or carrier to iMash: whitelist the signaling IP, configure inbound and outbound gateways, add authentication, and import your numbers.
